html:
RECOUP Integrative, Root-Cause Mold Toxicity Program

Introduction

  • Toxic Mold Illness is called Chronic Inflammatory Response Syndrome (CIRS).
  • Approximately 25-50% of buildings in America have significant mold growth.
  • Over 50 million Indians are affected by severe fungal disease, 10% of which are from potentially dangerous mold.
  • Approximately 25% of the population has a specific genetic markup that makes them vulnerable to CIRS.
  • While illnesses caused by biotoxins are on the rise, most physicians are unfamiliar with CIRS and their impact on the body. It's not unusual for someone with CIRS illness to go years or even decades without a proper diagnosis.

What is CIRS?

  • CIRS is a condition with a wide range of symptoms triggered by biotoxins—typically mold. The body's immune system is weakened or overactive.
  • Toxic mold is dangerous and can develop in water-damaged buildings (WDB). It can appear black, dark green, or invisible.
  • Mold overgrowth often colonizes the sinuses, lungs, or gastrointestinal tract.
  • Once exposed to mold, people become 'sensitized' and can develop an allergy-like reaction to dust, pet dander, old books, fragrances, and volatile organic compounds (VOCs).
  • Those infected with SARS-CoV-1 (Corona Virus) appear to have increased sensitization.
  • SIRS can cause symptoms ranging from mild to life-threatening.
  • The symptoms can start acutely or develop slowly over years.
  • CIRS can affect all genders and age groups.

Symptoms of SIRS

  • Feeling of ill health (flu-like)
  • A new sensitivity to environmental allergens
  • Histamine intolerance, hives, rashes
  • Numbness, tingling
  • Highly sensitive to supplements and medications
  • Chronic sinus congestion
  • Chronic ear congestion
  • Depression, panic attacks, anxiety
  • Shallow breathing/solar plexus tightness
  • Sneezing, hoarseness, coughing, congestion
  • Insomnia
  • Fainting
  • Seizures
  • Blood sugar regulation issues
  • Blood pressure regulation issues

The health consequences of SIRS

  • Acquired immune activation
  • Mood changes
  • Fatigue
  • Nausea
  • Headaches, dizziness
  • Digestive problems
  • Neurological problems
  • Fever, cough, and flu-like symptoms
  • Increased likelihood of asthma for exposed children
  • Profound hopelessness and despair

Assessment

  • RECOUP Entire Person Health Score and Care Plan Generation using AI: EPHS-S, EPHS-P, EPHS-O
  • Visual Contrast Test
  • Customized Laboratory Tests include urine mycotoxin levels, organic acid tests, and tests for inflammation, immune dysfunction and other organ dysfunction to determine if symptoms are due to toxicity, colonization, or a combination of both.

RECOUP's 4R approach for SIRS

It is based on targeted testing and tailored treatment.

Effective Relief:

  • Removal of the patient(s) from exposure.
  • Professional remediation.
  • Air Filtration
  • Anti-fungal and antimicrobial therapies for internal colonization by mold.
  • Optimizing sleep, anxiety and other acute symptoms
  • Nutritional assessment and interventions.
  • Control of inflammation in various parts of the body.
  • Addressing secretion of gastric acid, bile, enzymes
  • Ensuring adequate bowel movements

Root cause medicine:

  • Detoxification and elimination involve optimizing the function of the liver, kidneys, and gastrointestinal tract in processing and eliminating mycotoxins. Detoxification regimens include infrared sauna, nutrition, intravenous therapy, colon hydrotherapy, Ayurveda, naturopathy, and yoga, along with targeted treatments such as medicines, mycotoxin binders, and supplements.
  • Maintaining gut health and antioxidant support to reduce oxidative stress is essential to minimize adverse effects while detoxifying.
  • Helping the liver biotransformation of mold and mycotoxins and improving bile flow.
  • Addressing lifestyle factors including sleep, physical activity, stress, positive relationships and psychoemotional factors.
  • Mind-body approaches like Yoga, Tai Chi, Qi Gong, and Neurocognitive Imagery (Franklin Method).

Rebalancing body functions:

  • Restoration of nutrient deficiencies and electrolyte imbalances.
  • Correction of metabolic imbalances, e.g., HPA axis dysfunction.
  • Regulating the gut-brain axis with vagus nerve stimulation and heart rate variability biofeedback.
  • Physical rehabilitation to improve strength and aerobic conditioning.
  • Pulmonary rehabilitation for respiratory tract involvement.
  • Occupational therapy improves the ability to perform activities of daily living and work.

Building Inflammation Resilience:

Preserving optimal control on inflammatory response towards efficient repair of tissue and elimination of harmful toxins, without adverse effects, despite uncontrolled recurring/constant exposure to risk and harm using user-friendly care including health scores, exposure check, annual preventive health check, yearly wellness physical examination, personal calendar of heat and cold therapies, infrared sauna, restorative medical massages, microbiome replenishment, IV nutrition/antioxidants, regenerative therapies including ozone, hydrogen, peptides and several more.

Our Credentials

Dr Deepak Sharan is the only physician in Asia trained and certified in Dr Jill Crista's Mold Protocol (https://drcrista.com/doctor-finder/). The American Academy of Anti-Aging Medicine (Ritchie Shoemaker, Andrew Heyman) has also certified him in the Shoemaker Protocol for CIRS and Kharrazian Institute (Datis Kharrazian) in Chronic Infections.

Recoup Mold Toxicity Management Team:

  • Functional Medicine Physicians
  • Lifestyle Medicine Physicians
  • Rehabilitation Physicians
  • Occupational Health Physician
  • Other Specialist Physicians
  • Ayurveda Physicians
  • Naturopathy and Yoga Physicians
  • Nutritionists
  • Clinical Psychologists
  • Physiotherapists
  • Occupational Therapists
  • Aquatic Therapists
  • Health coaches
  • Nurses
  • Other professionals, as required
